qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of this document?
qa_pairs.answer: This document describes how to troubleshoot frequent issues when using our cloud service to operate a virtual machine, offering self-help solutions and guidance on submitting support tickets.

qa_pairs.question: What is a prerequisite for logging in to the cloud service?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to specifically apply for and be granted a cloud project to log in to our cloud service.

qa_pairs.question: What error message will I receive if I haven't been granted a cloud project when attempting to log in?
qa_pairs.answer: You will get the error message “Invalid Credentials” if you have not applied for and been granted a cloud project.

qa_pairs.question: How long should I expect to wait for my cloud project request to be approved?
qa_pairs.answer: It can take a few days for your cloud project request to be approved.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I haven't received my cloud project confirmation email after more than three business days?
qa_pairs.answer: Submit a ticket to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca with your name, institution, and the email address you used to submit the request.

qa_pairs.question: Which username format should I use when logging in to the cloud dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to use your username, the same as you would use to log in to an HPC cluster, and not your email address.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I reset my password if it is rejected when trying to log in to the cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: You can reset your password by visiting this link: https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/security/forgot.

qa_pairs.question: What information should I include when submitting a support ticket for cloud login issues after troubleshooting?
qa_pairs.answer: Include your username, project name, which cloud you are trying to access, and describe the steps you've taken so far.

qa_pairs.question: What are resource limits, also known as 'quotas,' in the context of launching a virtual machine?
qa_pairs.answer: Resource limits are restrictions on the number of VMs, CPUs, and GBs of RAM you can have in use at any given time within your cloud project.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check my current resource usage and limits for my cloud project?
qa_pairs.answer: Log in to your project cloud dashboard, then click 'Compute' and 'Overview' on the left-side navigation menu.

qa_pairs.question: How do I request more resources if my VM launch fails due to exceeding my quotas?
qa_pairs.answer: You can request more resources using the provided form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeU_BoRk5cEz3AvVLf3e9yZJq-OvcFCQ-mg7p4AWXmUkd5rTw/viewform.

qa_pairs.question: What is a common cause of the 'Error: No valid host was found' message when launching a VM?
qa_pairs.answer: Manually selecting an inappropriate Availability Zone instead of using the 'Any Availability Zone' default option is a common cause.

qa_pairs.question: How should I resolve the 'Error: No valid host was found' if it's due to an Availability Zone selection?
qa_pairs.answer: Fix this by setting the Availability Zone back to 'Any Availability Zone'.

qa_pairs.question: What information should I provide when submitting a ticket for VM launch failures, especially with the 'not enough hosts' error?
qa_pairs.answer: Provide the cloud name, project name, the steps you have taken thus far, and the reason why you need a particular availability zone if applicable.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step to troubleshoot if I can't connect to my virtual machine or its services?
qa_pairs.answer: Check the System Status page to see if there is an incident affecting service on your cloud.

qa_pairs.question: If I can reach the login page for my cloud dashboard but cannot log in, what should I do?
qa_pairs.answer: Please see the 'Can’t login to Cloud' guide in the previous section on this page.

qa_pairs.question: After logging into the cloud dashboard, how can I check if my VM is running?
qa_pairs.answer: Navigate to the Instances screen on the left-side menu and look at the Power State for your VM; it should be 'Running'.

qa_pairs.question: If my VM is not in the 'Running' state, how can I try to restart it?
qa_pairs.answer: Use the 'Actions' menu on the right-hand side of the Instances screen and select 'Start Instance' or 'Resume Instance'.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find a log of actions applied to my VM to investigate unrecognized changes?
qa_pairs.answer: From the Instances screen, click on your Instance name (VM name) and then click on the 'Action Log' tab.

qa_pairs.question: What details are required if I can't restart my VM and need to submit a support ticket?
qa_pairs.answer: Include your name, username, hosting cloud, project name, VM ID, the issue you are seeing, and the troubleshooting steps taken so far.

qa_pairs.question: What is the default username for connecting to an AlmaLinux VM via SSH?
qa_pairs.answer: The default username for an AlmaLinux VM is 'almalinux'.

qa_pairs.question: How can I verify my current IP address when troubleshooting SSH connection issues to my VM?
qa_pairs.answer: Open https://ipv4.icanhazip.com/ in a browser to check your IP address.

qa_pairs.question: What security group rule is necessary to allow SSH connections to my VM?
qa_pairs.answer: There should be an 'Ingress' rule with IP Protocol 'TCP', Port Range '22 (SSH)', and Remote IP Prefix set to your-ip-address/32.

qa_pairs.question: When submitting a ticket because I still can't connect to my instance via SSH, what crucial identifier should I include?
qa_pairs.answer: Include the instance UUID, along with the cloud name, project name, and all information collected from the troubleshooting steps.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary condition that must be met before a volume can be deleted?
qa_pairs.answer: You cannot delete a volume that is attached to a running VM; it needs to be detached first.

qa_pairs.question: After detaching a volume, what status should it show before it can be deleted?
qa_pairs.answer: The volume should be listed as 'Available'; if it's still 'In-use', a ticket should be submitted.

qa_pairs.question: What must be done with snapshots related to a volume before the source volume can be deleted?
qa_pairs.answer: You must delete any snapshots of the volume before you can delete the source volume.

qa_pairs.question: What information should I include in a support ticket if I am unable to delete a volume after following the steps?
qa_pairs.answer: Include your username, the project name, the cloud name, the UUID of the volume, and any other information collected during troubleshooting.
